commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=87fb3441b1227efede1e11d5cfe86642f1f96ee6)
commands.review_summary:
The PR forwards the `chat.send` session id into reply initialization and reuses a stale session-store entry when that requested id matches, with gateway and session regression tests.

PR surface: Source +10, Tests +60. Total +70 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main has Control UI sending the current session id on `chat.send`, while reply initialization ignores it and stale entries roll over through `initSessionState`. I did not run the Mac sleep/Docker/Chrome reproduction live.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The blocker is a session lifecycle policy/compatibility decision, not a safe mechanical autofix on the contributor branch.

target.title: fix #87700: [Bug]: Control UI webchat session silently resets after network disconnect / sleep — previous session file renamed to .jsonl.reset.* and lost from sessions tab
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Control UI webchat could keep showing an existing conversation after reconnect, but the next send could be treated as an expired session and roll over the transcript.
- Solution: carry the existing `chat.send` `sessionId` into reply initialization and reuse the current store entry when the client asserts the same session id.
- What changed: `requestedSessionId` now flows from gateway chat send options into `initSessionState`, with gateway and session regression tests.
- What did NOT change: explicit `/new` or idle expiry without a matching client session id still creates a new session.
- Fixes #87700

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** after a sleep/disconnect cycle, Control UI can still hold and send the currently displayed session id, but the gateway reply initialization previously ignored that id and could treat an idle-expired entry as stale, creating a new session and renaming the old transcript.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux local OpenClaw worktree on latest `origin/main` (`9cb052ccef`) with Node/Vitest via the repository `scripts/run-vitest.mjs` wrapper, plus a `node --import tsx` runtime proof that imports the patched session lifecycle code and writes a real temporary `sessions.json` store.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```sh
  node --import tsx "$EVIDENCE_DIR/session-reconnect-proof.mjs"
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts
  git diff --check HEAD~1 HEAD
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```text
  withoutRequested= {"isNewSession":true,"sessionId":"bc5fbc96-4063-4f0b-a346-e96dcb18251a","hasPreviousSessionEntry":true}
  withRequested= {"isNewSession":false,"sessionId":"sess-existing-webchat","hasPreviousSessionEntry":false}
  proof= requested matching session id prevents idle rollover for current webchat session

  [test] starting test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests  29 passed (29)

  [test] starting test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply.config.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests  97 passed (97)

  [test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 96.42s
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** with no requested session id, the same stale store entry still creates a new session and exposes a previous session entry for rollover; with the matching requested id from the reconnecting webchat client, the existing session id is reused and there is no previous session entry to roll over. The gateway test also proves `chat.send` forwards `sessionId` into reply initialization.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run a live Mac sleep/Docker/Chrome reproduction; the runtime proof covers the session lifecycle behavior against a real store file, and the gateway test covers the request handoff that feeds that lifecycle decision.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts` and `src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** gateway `chat.send` preserves the caller-provided `sessionId`, and the session initializer reuses an idle-expired entry only when that requested id matches the current entry.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** the issue is caused by the handoff between the webchat gateway request and session reset policy, so the tests cover both the boundary wiring and the reset decision without requiring a browser sleep simulation.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** `chat.send` already parsed the client-supplied `sessionId`, but reply initialization did not receive it. After a disconnect or sleep window, `initSessionState` only evaluated the stored entry against idle/daily freshness, so a still-displayed webchat session could be marked stale on the next message and the previous transcript could be rolled into a `.reset.*` file.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** the gateway now carries the current client session assertion into the single session lifecycle decision point. `initSessionState` reuses only the existing entry whose stored `sessionId` exactly matches the requested id, so reconnecting webchat sends preserve continuity while stale or mismatched requests still follow the normal reset path.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High
- **Patch quality notes:** The `return undefined` lines are mock reply implementations in tests so `chat.send` can reach and expose the captured options without running a real model call; they are not production fallbacks or symptom masking.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The session store remains the source of truth. This patch does not add a new session store or config surface; it passes an already-existing gateway request field into the existing session lifecycle owner.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
